core_mechanism: Axiomatic aggregation of a profile of individual threshold (pass/fail against a fixed family of sufficiency levels) classifications into a single complete preorder (ranking), uniquely pinned down by a set of consistency axioms and implementable by a strategy-proof majority/median voting protocol.
brief_reason: The abstract builds grading rules from a finite family of threshold ('sufficientarian') judgements on individuals, characterizes the total preorders they induce, and shows a class of unanimity-respecting, strategy-proof protocols (simple majority for odd agent counts) selects one specific rule — i.e., an axiomatically characterized, strategy-proof aggregation of threshold classifications into a ranking.
